problem_74.natural_language: Rank inequalities for a composition `f ∘ g` when `dim V = n`.
problem_74.lean:
theorem rank_comp_bounds
    {K U V W : Type*} [Field K]
    [AddCommGroup U] [Module K U]
    [AddCommGroup V] [Module K V] [FiniteDimensional K V]
    [AddCommGroup W] [Module K W]
    (f : V →ₗ[K] W) (g : U →ₗ[K] V) (n : Nat)
    (hn : Module.finrank K V = n) :
    Module.finrank K (LinearMap.range f) + Module.finrank K (LinearMap.range g) - n
        ≤ Module.finrank K (LinearMap.range (f.comp g)) ∧
      Module.finrank K (LinearMap.range (f.comp g)) ≤
        min (Module.finrank K (LinearMap.range f)) (Module.finrank K (LinearMap.range g))
problem_74.natural_language_proof:
Proof: for the upper bound, `range (f ∘ g)` is contained in `range f`, so
`rank(f ∘ g) ≤ rank f`. Also `range (f ∘ g) = f '' (range g)`, so its dimension is at most that of
`range g`, hence `rank(f ∘ g) ≤ rank g`. Therefore `rank(f ∘ g) ≤ min(rank f, rank g)`.

For the lower bound, restrict `f` to `range g`. Then
its range is exactly `range (f ∘ g)`, so
`rank(f ∘ g) = dim(range g) - dim(ker f ∩ range g)`.
We have `dim(ker f ∩ range g) ≤ dim(ker f)`. By rank-nullity for `f : V -> W`,
`dim(ker f) = n - dim(range f)`. Hence
`rank(f ∘ g) ≥ dim(range g) - (n - dim(range f)) = dim(range f) + dim(range g) - n`,
which is the desired inequality.
